records.ety: [From Staff, and corresponding to the pl. staves. See Staff.]

defs: One of a number of narrow strips of wood, or narrow iron plates, placed edge to edge to form the sides, covering, or lining of a vessel or structure; esp., one of the strips which form the sides of a cask, a pail, etc.
One of the cylindrical bars of a lantern wheel; one of the bars or rounds of a rack, a ladder, etc.
A metrical portion; a stanza; a staff.
quotes: Let us chant a passing stave In honor of that hero brave.

defs: The five horizontal and parallel lines on and between which musical notes are written or printed; the staff{7}.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Staved (stāvd) or Stove (stōv); p. pr. & vb. n. Staving.]

defs: To break in a stave or the staves of; to break a hole in; to burst; -- often with in; as, to stave a cask; to stave in a boat.
To push, as with a staff; -- with off.
quotes: The condition of a servant staves him off to a distance.

defs: To delay by force or craft; to drive away; -- usually with off; as, to stave off the execution of a project.
quotes: And answered with such craft as women use, Guilty or guiltless, to stave off a chance That breaks upon them perilously.

defs: To suffer, or cause, to be lost by breaking the cask.
quotes: All the wine in the city has been staved.

defs: To furnish with staves or rundles.
To render impervious or solid by driving with a calking iron; as, to stave lead, or the joints of pipes into which lead has been run.
To burst in pieces by striking against something; to dash into fragments.
quotes: Like a vessel of glass she stove and sank.
